key_themes: AI chip partnerships reshaping smartphone and memory landscape
Mixed Q1 earnings — consumer/pizza disappoints, telecoms and steel beat
Iran-Strait of Hormuz tensions cap equity gains; oil pushes higher

stocks.reason: Surged 12-13% after reports that OpenAI is partnering with Qualcomm and MediaTek to develop AI smartphone processors targeting 300-400M annual units for a 2028 device launch
Plunged ~46% after Marvell Technology (which acquired Celestial AI) cancelled all purchase orders citing alleged confidentiality violations by POET regarding public order disclosures
Fell ~10% after Q1 US same-store sales grew only 0.9%, badly missing the 2.3% analyst consensus; EPS also disappointed
Gained ~3.5% after Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.28 beat $1.20 estimate; company raised full-year 2026 earnings guidance
Surged ~5% after Melius Research initiated with a Buy rating citing 41% upside; analyst highlighted sustained HBM demand powering AI data centers through 2030
Jumped 8.1% to fresh all-time highs as Melius memory-demand note lifted the sector; stock is up 3,000%+ over the past year on AI storage tailwinds
Upgraded to Buy from Neutral by Redburn (Rothschild & Co) with PT raised to $10 from $5; analyst cited first-ever GAAP profitability and Spectacles-driven core-business breakout potential

Downgraded to Neutral from Buy by Redburn; analyst argued Pinterest is "doubling down on the same approach that has failed it" and faces the least revenue-diversification potential among social peers amid AI-driven ad-market bifurcation
Slid ~3% after BNP Paribas Exane downgraded to Neutral from Outperform with $1,190 PT; analyst noted 90% of gas turbine capacity already contracted through 2030, limiting further upside catalysts
Surged 12% after hours after Q1 EPS of $0.44 beat $0.36 estimate; net interest margin of 6.28% topped 6.06% consensus, signaling strong lending spread performance
Added ~4% after hours after Q1 EPS of $3.23 beat $2.82 estimate; revenue of $9.50B exceeded $8.88B expectation, signaling steel demand resilience
Fell ~10% after hours as Q1 adjusted operating margin came in at 42%, down from 46% a year ago, disappointing investors expecting margin expansion in the memory IP cycle
Jumped ~9% after Q1 revenue of $4.92B slightly beat estimates; adjusted loss narrowed sharply to $0.40/share from $0.93 a year ago; management guided for Q2 profitability swing
Gained 4.45% on sustained AI chip demand optimism; named in multiple Buy-reiterations on Monday as the dominant GPU provider for data-center HBM workloads heading into Mag-7 earnings week
Jefferies initiated with Buy rating, calling Rubrik well-positioned for long-term growth driven by ransomware proliferation, SaaS/cloud workload expansion, and secular data-growth tailwinds
